meta.name: Mal MSB — Compliance Risk Typology Register
meta.basis: MSB_Compliance_Risk_Typology_Register.xlsx — internal working draft compiled to best knowledge, no live web access; every FATF rating, threshold, retention period and NRA finding is time-sensitive and marked [VERIFY] in the source. Incorporates the US 2026 Treasury NMLRA / NTFRA / NPFRA, Pakistan NRA 2023 and FATF/Egmont MSB typologies.
headline: Fraud — IC3 2024
859,532 complaints, >$16B losses (+33% YoY)
2026 NMLRA
Pig-butchering / digital-asset scams
$5.8B (+47%), run from SE-Asia scam centres
2026 NMLRA
Investment fraud (overall)
$6.57B (IC3 2024, +44%)
2026 NMLRA
DPRK digital-asset heists
Bybit ~$1.5B (Feb 2025) — top PF threat
2026 NPFRA
Iran 'shadow banking' shells
~$4.2B moved (FinCEN Oct 2025)
2026 NPFRA
Hizballah financing
~$700M/yr (historical) via fronts & exchange houses
2026 NTFRA

typologies.name: Hawala / hundi informal remittance
typologies.desc: Value moved outside the formal banking channel via informal brokers; major risk given large diaspora.
typologies.flags: Inbound/outbound flows with no corresponding bank settlement; customer references 'agent' for delivery; mismatched sender/receiver relationship; round-amount FX.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Trade-based ML (over/under-invoicing)
typologies.desc: Mis-priced or phantom RMG/import-export trade used to move value.
typologies.flags: Invoice value inconsistent with goods/market price; payments to third-party not on invoice; goods routed via high-risk transit.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Inbound remittance layering
typologies.desc: Legitimate migrant-worker remittance corridors abused to inject illicit funds.
typologies.flags: Remittance volume inconsistent with stated occupation; many senders to one beneficiary; rapid cash-out.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Mobile financial services structuring
typologies.desc: bKash/Nagad-type wallets & agent networks used for smurfing.
typologies.flags: Many small loads just under reporting/KYC tiers; rapid wallet-to-wallet hops; agent self-dealing.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: NPO / charity abuse for TF
typologies.desc: Charitable conduits used to raise/move funds for terrorism.
typologies.flags: Donations routed to conflict regions; opaque beneficiaries; cash-intensive collections.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: PEP / corruption proceeds
typologies.desc: Embezzlement and bribery proceeds laundered domestically and offshore.
typologies.flags: Government-linked customer; wealth inconsistent with role; use of family nominees.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Parallel-market FX / currency smuggling
typologies.desc: Black-market currency dealing and cross-border cash given FX controls.
typologies.flags: Demand for large cash FX; rates off official; structuring around FX limits.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Hawala informal transfer
typologies.flags: No bank settlement leg; 'agent' delivery references.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Trade-based ML
typologies.flags: Price/quantity mismatch; third-party payers; transit via high-risk ports.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Remittance-corridor abuse
typologies.desc: Large worker-remittance inflows used to layer funds.
typologies.flags: Sender/beneficiary mismatch; many-to-one patterns.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Antiquities / cultural-property proceeds
typologies.desc: Smuggled antiquities monetised through the system.
typologies.flags: Dealings in cultural goods; cash settlement; vague provenance.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Regional terrorist financing
typologies.desc: Funds raised/moved for regional terrorist activity.
typologies.flags: Transfers to conflict-adjacent areas; cash collection networks.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Corruption / PEP proceeds
typologies.flags: State-linked customers; unexplained wealth.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Cyber-enabled fraud & investment scams
typologies.desc: Scam proceeds funnelled through mule accounts/wallets.
typologies.flags: Rapid in-out; many unrelated payers; accounts opened then immediately funded & drained; device/IP anomalies.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Illegal online gambling proceeds
typologies.desc: Unlicensed gambling settled through MSBs and e-wallets.
typologies.flags: Frequent small credits from gaming-linked sources; payouts to many beneficiaries.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Grand corruption (1MDB-type) layering
typologies.desc: Misappropriated public funds layered via shells & offshore.
typologies.flags: Complex layering; offshore SPVs; high-value assets.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Trade-based ML / free-zone abuse
typologies.flags: Price mismatch; circular trade; FTZ re-routing.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Drug-trafficking proceeds (regional transit)
typologies.flags: Cash-heavy; border-region nexus.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Real estate & luxury-goods laundering
typologies.flags: Third-party funding; rapid resale; under/over-valuation.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Cross-border worker remittance abuse
typologies.flags: Sender/beneficiary mismatch; many-to-one.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Cash economy & structuring
typologies.desc: Large informal cash economy enables smurfing.
typologies.flags: Repeated sub-threshold cash; layered deposits.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Hawala-type informal transfer
typologies.flags: No bank settlement; 'agent' delivery.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Real estate & tourism laundering
typologies.flags: Third-party funding; valuation anomalies.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Regional / Sahel terrorist financing
typologies.flags: Transfers toward Sahel; cash collection.
typologies.src: FATF/FSRB MER & follow-up; national NRA; regulator guidance

typologies.name: Remittance-corridor abuse
typologies.desc: Diaspora remittances used to layer funds.
typologies.flags: Sender/beneficiary mismatch.

crossborder.name: Trade-based money laundering
crossborder.jurs: Bangladesh, Egypt, Malaysia, Morocco, Pakistan, Singapore, United Arab Emirates
crossborder.why: Mis-invoiced trade moves value across borders through transit hubs (Singapore, UAE) into and out of manufacturing/import economies.
crossborder.flags: Invoice price/quantity inconsistent with market; third-party (non-invoice) payers; circular or transit trade; round-tripping.

crossborder.name: Remittance-corridor abuse
crossborder.jurs: Bangladesh, Egypt, Malaysia, Morocco, Pakistan, Philippines
crossborder.why: Migrant-worker corridors connect sending hubs (UAE, Singapore, Malaysia) with receiving economies (Bangladesh, Pakistan, Philippines, Egypt, Morocco).
crossborder.flags: Remittance volume inconsistent with stated occupation; many senders to one beneficiary; rapid cash-out; new corridor with no profile.

crossborder.name: Terrorist / proliferation financing
crossborder.jurs: Bangladesh, Egypt, Morocco, Pakistan, Singapore, United Arab Emirates
crossborder.why: Funds are raised and moved toward conflict-adjacent regions; charity conduits and cash collection cross borders.
crossborder.flags: Transfers toward listed/conflict regions; charity conduits; small structured collections; sanctioned-nexus counterparties.

crossborder.name: Corruption & PEP proceeds
crossborder.jurs: Bangladesh, Egypt, Philippines, Singapore, United Arab Emirates
crossborder.why: Foreign PEP and corruption proceeds are routed into hub markets (UAE, Singapore) from across the region.
crossborder.flags: PEP or state-linked nexus; unexplained wealth/inflows; use of family nominees; reluctance to evidence source of wealth.

crossborder.name: Hawala / informal value transfer (IVTS)
crossborder.jurs: Bangladesh, Egypt, Morocco, Pakistan, United Arab Emirates
crossborder.why: Informal value-transfer networks span sender and receiver countries with no settling bank leg; corridors interconnect South Asia, MENA and the Gulf.
crossborder.flags: No corresponding bank settlement; 'agent will deliver' references; mismatched sender/receiver; round-amount FX; corridor concentration.

crossborder.name: Cash structuring (smurfing)
crossborder.why: Bulk cash and structuring feed into informal corridors and are carried across borders.
crossborder.flags: Repeated sub-threshold cash; multiple branches/agents; rapid aggregation then transfer.

crossborder.name: Real estate & high-value assets
crossborder.jurs: Malaysia, Morocco, Singapore, United Arab Emirates
crossborder.why: Proceeds are parked in property/luxury assets in hub markets (UAE, Singapore, Malaysia) regardless of origin.
crossborder.flags: Third-party/foreign funding; cash component; rapid purchase/resale; under- or over-valuation.

crossborder.name: Shell companies / complex structures
crossborder.why: Opaque structures and free-zone/offshore entities layer proceeds across multiple jurisdictions.
crossborder.flags: Multiple SPVs; nominee directors; circular ownership; free-zone entity with no substance; opaque beneficial owner.

crossborder.name: Cyber fraud, scams & money mules
crossborder.why: Scam syndicates operate across borders; mule networks and wallets move proceeds between jurisdictions rapidly.
crossborder.flags: Account funded then immediately drained; many unrelated payers; device/IP anomalies; victim-pattern inbound credits.

crossborder.name: Online / illegal gambling
crossborder.why: Online/illegal gaming operations and payouts cross borders (notably Philippines and Malaysia).
crossborder.flags: Gaming-linked counterparties; bulk payouts to many beneficiaries; chip purchase with minimal play.

crossborder.name: Gold & precious metals
crossborder.why: Gold/DPMS store and move value across the Gulf and Asian trade routes.
crossborder.flags: Bullion/cash-for-gold settlement; weight/value mismatch; rapid resale.

crossborder.name: Virtual-asset / crypto layering
crossborder.why: Virtual assets are inherently cross-border; layering and off-ramping move between regulated (Singapore, UAE, Malaysia, Philippines) and restricted markets.
crossborder.flags: Bursty on/off-ramp activity; unhosted-wallet exposure; chain-hopping; mismatch with customer profile; travel-rule gaps.

segments.method: Practice or client accounts used to move or layer third-party funds.
segments.flags: Client/pooled-account flows inconsistent with the practice; third-party funds passing through; payments unrelated to the profession.

segments.method: Undeclared income routed through professional accounts.
segments.flags: Income inconsistent with declared profession; structured cash; offshore movement.

segments.typ: Money-mule recruitment via fake remote roles
segments.method: ‘Job’ requires receiving and forwarding funds, masking origin.
segments.flags: Inbound from many unrelated payers then forwarded; employer/geography mismatch; rapid in-out; reluctance to explain.

segments.method: Account funded by parties unrelated to stated employment.
segments.flags: Funder ≠ stated employer; salary pattern absent; sudden volume change.

segments.method: One account used to receive and remit on behalf of many.
segments.flags: One account receiving for many senders; remittances exceeding wages; third-party control of the account; coercion signs.

segments.method: Account interfaces with informal value transfer or holds trafficking/exploitation proceeds.
segments.flags: No bank settlement leg; corridor concentration; recruitment-fee-linked flows; account controlled by a third party.

segments.method: Invoices without real deliverables used to move value.
segments.flags: Invoices with no evidence of work; round-sum recurring from unrelated parties; income inconsistent with history; rapid withdrawal.

segments.method: Account used to receive and forward third-party funds.
segments.flags: Inbound then immediate outbound; unrelated payers; profile mismatch.

segments.typ: Mule pass-through / structuring of unexplained side-inflows
segments.method: Salaried account used for unrelated third-party throughput.
segments.flags: Salary credits plus unexplained third-party inflows; sudden volume change; forwarding behaviour.

segments.typ: Collusive / fake-gig self-laundering via platform payouts
segments.method: Self-dealing or collusive buyer-seller gigs used to legitimise funds.
segments.flags: Platform payouts inconsistent with account history; circular payments; clients in high-risk jurisdictions; rapid cash-out / crypto off-ramp.

segments.method: Payments from clients in sanctioned / high-risk locations.
segments.flags: Counterparties in sanctioned or high-risk jurisdictions; IP/geo mismatch; payment routing anomalies.

segments.method: Personal and business funds mixed to obscure illicit cash.
segments.flags: Cash deposits inconsistent with the business; round-number patterns; third-party funding; rapid movement out.

segments.method: Over/under-invoicing used to move value across borders.
segments.flags: Invoice value vs goods/market mismatch; third-party payers; high-risk counterparties.

segments.method: Vulnerable customers recruited to receive/forward funds or rent accounts.
segments.flags: Activity inconsistent with pension/student profile; sudden high-volume throughput; third-party control; inbound from unrelated parties then forwarded.

segments.method: Fiat↔crypto conversion used to layer; exposure to mixers / high-risk VASPs.
segments.flags: Frequent fiat↔crypto conversion; exposure to mixers / sanctioned wallets; chain-hopping; structuring around VASP limits.

segments.method: Crypto channels used to move fraud/ransomware proceeds or evade sanctions.
segments.flags: Inbound from scam/ransomware-linked addresses; sanctioned-wallet exposure; bursty on/off-ramp; victim-pattern flows.

corridors.profile: Large diaspora & migrant-worker remittance corridor; cash-out heavy at agents.
corridors.typ: Hundi/hawala diversion of formal remittance; trade-based ML (RMG/import-export); inbound-remittance layering; NPO/charity TF; MFS (bKash/Nagad) structuring.
corridors.flags: Sender income inconsistent with volume; many senders → one beneficiary; rapid agent cash-out; payments linked to garment trade; beneficiary near conflict/charity nexus.
corridors.actors: Hundi/hawala networks; trade mis-invoicing rings; labour-recruitment fraud; charity-linked TF.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala & Other Similar Service Providers’ (2013); FinCEN Human Trafficking advisory (2014/2020…

corridors.profile: Diaspora remittance into a cash-heavy, FX-controlled economy.
corridors.typ: Parallel-market FX / currency smuggling; hawala settlement; structuring to evade FX limits; trade-based ML.
corridors.flags: Pressure to convert to USD cash; sender/beneficiary mismatch; transfers timed to FX-rate gaps; rapid cash withdrawal.
corridors.actors: Hawala / parallel-FX dealers; trade mis-invoicing; (rarely) antiquities proceeds.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); MENAFATF Egypt MER; FinCEN BEC advisory (2016/2019).

corridors.profile: Transit & scam-syndicate hub in SE Asia; growing e-wallet/crypto use.
corridors.typ: Cyber-scam proceeds (pig-butchering/romance/investment) via mules; online-gambling settlement; virtual-asset on/off-ramp layering; TBML / free-zone.
corridors.flags: Inbound to newly-opened mule accounts then onward; links to scam-compound regions; crypto bridging; gaming-linked counterparties.
corridors.actors: SE-Asian cyber-scam syndicates (often using trafficked labour); illegal online gambling; drug-transit launderers.
corridors.adv: FinCEN ‘pig-butchering’/online-scam Alert (Sept 2023); FinCEN Ransomware/CVC advisory (2020/2021); FinCEN Hum…

corridors.profile: Diaspora remittance; cash economy; Sahel TF adjacency.
corridors.typ: Hawala-type informal transfer; cash structuring; drug (cannabis) proceeds; regional/Sahel TF; real-estate placement.
corridors.flags: Sender mismatch; cash-out near border regions; transfers to high-risk adjacency; rapid property-linked payouts.
corridors.actors: Hawala networks; cannabis-trafficking launderers; smuggling rings.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); MENAFATF Morocco MER; FATF ‘Emerging Terrorist Financing Risks’ (2015).

corridors.profile: One of the largest global remittance corridors; hawala/hundi dominant; elevated TF/PF.
corridors.typ: Hundi/hawala diversion; terrorist financing to designated groups; proliferation-financing nexus; charity/NPO abuse; TBML.
corridors.flags: Many senders → one beneficiary; charity-linked transfers; sends to high-risk regions; hundi (no settlement leg); sanctioned-nexus counterparties.
corridors.actors: Hundi networks; designated-group TF facilitators; PF front companies; smuggling/TBML rings.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); FATF Pakistan MER & action-plan history; FinCEN/OFAC sanctions-screening ex…

corridors.profile: Huge OFW remittance corridor; scam-syndicate & online-gaming exposure.
corridors.typ: OFW-remittance pattern abuse; cyber-scam (pig-butchering/romance) mule layering; POGO/online-gaming settlement; casino-linked ML; human-trafficking proceeds.
corridors.flags: Many-to-one beneficiary patterns abused; victim-pattern inbound then rapid cash-out; gaming-linked credits; new accounts drained quickly.
corridors.actors: Cyber-scam syndicates; POGO-linked launderers; casino junket networks; trafficking rings.
corridors.adv: FinCEN ‘pig-butchering’/online-scam Alert (Sept 2023); FinCEN Human Trafficking advisory (2014/2020); FinCEN…

corridors.profile: Major financial hub / pass-through point; high-value & crypto exposure.
corridors.typ: Layering of foreign proceeds; shell / complex-structure pass-through; high-value-asset & property placement; virtual-asset flows; sanctions/PF transit.
corridors.flags: Pass-through with no economic rationale; complex/nominee structures; foreign-PEP inflows; immediate onward high-value transfer.
corridors.actors: Foreign-proceeds layering networks; sanctions/PF transit facilitators; professional enablers.
corridors.adv: FATF Singapore MER; FinCEN/OFAC Iran (2018) & DPRK (2017) illicit-finance advisories; Russia export-control e…

corridors.profile: Major re-export / trade & gold hub; hawala & sanctions-evasion exposure.
corridors.typ: Trade-based ML (re-export); gold/precious-metals value transfer; real-estate placement; free-zone shell misuse; hawala; sanctions/PF evasion (onward to Iran).
corridors.flags: Onward transfer to higher-risk jurisdictions; gold/trade settlement; free-zone entity with no substance; sanctioned-nexus or dual-use goods.
corridors.actors: TBML & gold-laundering rings; sanctions-evasion / re-export facilitators; hawala networks.
corridors.adv: FATF UAE MER & grey-list history; FinCEN/OFAC Iran illicit-finance advisory (2018); Russia export-control eva…

corridors.profile: Top-tier real-world remittance & hawala corridor; very high TF/hawala density.
corridors.typ: Hawala/hundi settlement; TF to high-risk regions; TBML/gold; bulk-cash & re-export linkage.
corridors.flags: No bank settlement leg; many-to-one; charity/high-risk-region nexus; gold/trade settlement.
corridors.actors: Hawala networks; TF facilitators; TBML/gold rings.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); FATF Pakistan & UAE MERs; FATF ‘Emerging TF Risks’ (2015).

corridors.typ: Hawala diversion; TBML (RMG); inbound-remittance layering; labour-migration fraud nexus.
corridors.flags: Sender mismatch; many-to-one; rapid cash-out; trade-linked payments.
corridors.actors: Hawala networks; trade mis-invoicing; recruitment-fraud rings.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); FATF–Egmont TBML (2020); FinCEN Human Trafficking advisory.

corridors.typ: Cyber-scam mule layering; online-gaming/POGO settlement; OFW-pattern abuse; crypto bridging.
corridors.flags: Mule-account inbound then onward; gaming-linked; victim-pattern flows; crypto on/off-ramp.
corridors.actors: Cyber-scam syndicates; POGO launderers; trafficking rings.
corridors.adv: FinCEN ‘pig-butchering’ Alert (Sept 2023); FinCEN Ransomware/CVC advisory; APG Philippines MER.

corridors.typ: Hawala settlement; parallel-FX (Egypt); cash structuring; trade-based ML.
corridors.flags: FX-gap timing; cash-out; sender mismatch; trade-linked settlement.
corridors.adv: FATF, ‘Role of Hawala...’ (2013); MENAFATF Egypt & Morocco MERs.

jurProfiles.fw: Money Laundering Prevention Act 2012 (amended); Anti-Terrorism Act 2009; BFIU AML/CFT Guidelines & Master Circular
jurProfiles.fatf: Assessed by APG (3rd-round MER ~2016 + follow-ups). Not on the FATF increased-monitoring list per last knowledge. [VERIFY current MER ratings & follow-up status]
jurProfiles.defi: Common themes: use of financial intelligence & ML prosecution outcomes; TBML detection; NPO/charity oversight; beneficial-ownership transparency. [VERIFY against published MER]
